text: Action: During a conflict, bow this stronghold – each defending character you control gets +1[conflict-military] and +1[conflict-political] until the end of the conflict.
name: Shiro Nishiyama

rulings.text: Shiro Nishiyama only affects characters that are already participating in the conflict. Any characters that enter the conflict after the ability has resolved are not affected.

text: Action: During a [conflict-political] conflict, bow this stronghold. Choose a participating character with [conflict-political] skill 2 or lower – bow that character.
name: Shizuka Toshi

text: Action: During a conflict, bow this stronghold. Choose a participating character with 1 or more attachments on it – until the end of the conflict, that character gets +1[conflict-military] and +1[conflict-political] (+2[conflict-military] and +2[conflict-political] instead if it has 2 or more attachments on it).
name: Mountain's Anvil Castle

rulings.text: The bonus provided by Mountain's Anvil Castle is fixed upon resolution. If the number of attachments subsequently changes, the bonus is not affected.

text: Action: During a conflict, bow this stronghold – each attacking character you control gets +1[conflict-military] until the end of the conflict.
name: Yōjin no Shiro

rulings.text: Yōjin no Shiro only affects characters that are already attacking. Any characters that enter the conflict after the ability has resolved are not affected.

errata.text: [Sample errata] Yōjin no Shiro: While you have at least one ready character with military skill 3 or higher, this stronghold gains "Reaction: After a unit declares an attack against this province — bow this card to bow the attacking character."

text: Action: Bow this stronghold, choose a character – that character gets +2 glory until the end of the phase.
name: Isawa Mori Seidō

rulings.text: Glory count happens before the conflict phase ends, so Isawa Mori Seidō can help you claim the Imperial Favor.

text: Action: If you are less honorable than an opponent, bow this stronghold – gain 1 honor. Errata from RRG v16 (2021-01-05)
name: City of the Open Hand

rulings.text:
City of the Open Hand received official errata. 

Its ability now instructs you to either gain 1 honor, or force your opponent to lose 1 honor.

text: Action: During a [conflict-military] conflict, bow this stronghold. Choose a Cavalry character you control – move it to that conflict.
name: Golden Plains Outpost

text: Reaction: After you win a conflict at this province – resolve the ring effect of that conflict as if you were the attacking player.
name: Defend the Wall

text: Interrupt: When this province is broken – dishonor each attacking character and honor each defending character.
flavorText: "One does not always need to kill to be victorious." - Kakita's The Sword
name: The Art of Peace

text: Interrupt: When this province is broken – your opponent discards cards from his or her hand until that player has 4 or fewer cards in hand. Errata from RRG v9
name: Restoration of Balance

rulings.text: Cards are discarded all at once. You cannot discard down to fewer than 4 cards.

text: Interrupt: When this province is broken – draw 3 cards.
flavorText: "Let your enemy see what he wishes to see, so that he will underestimate you." - Akodo's Leadership
name: The Art of War

text: Cannot be a stronghold province. Action: During a conflict at this province, select one – switch the contested ring with an unclaimed ring (the conflict retains its type), or switch the conflict type (the conflict retains its element). Errata from Imperial Law (2020-04-06).
name: Kuroi Mori

rulings.text: When the conflict type is switched, any characters that cannot participate in the conflict of the new type (have a dash (–) for a skill, or have Pacifism attached) immediately go home bowed.
Once the new ring becomes the contested ring, the attacking player takes all fate on it.
Changing the conflict type does not affect what other conflicts the attacker can declare. What matters is which conflict type was originally declared.

text: Reaction: After a conflict is declared against this province – search the top 5 cards of your conflict deck for a card and add it to your hand. Shuffle.
name: Secret Cache

rulings.text: If you use Secret Cache to find an event card with a reaction to a conflict being declared (e.g. [Mantra of Fire](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/mantra-of-fire)), you can play it during the same reaction window.

If [Chasing the Sun](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/chasing-the-sun) or [Talisman of the Sun](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/talisman-of-the-sun) are used to move the contested ring to Secret Cache, the province's ability cannot be triggered, because an attack is not being declared.

text: Reaction: After an attack is declared here, break this province. Your opponent chooses an attacking character – discard that character.
name: Endless Plains

rulings.text:
If Endless Plains is broken to discard the only attacker, the conflict does not end immediately, but rather continues as normal. The defending player can declare defenders, and both players can play characters from hand into the conflict, or use card effects to put characters into play or move them to the conflict.

However, if both players count 0 skill during conflict resolution, the conflict resolves with no winner, and the contested ring is returned to the pool of unclaimed rings.

In a Unicorn mirror match, if Endless Plains breaks as a cost of its ability ([step 4](https://fiveringsdb.com/rules/reference#initiating-abilities-playing-cards)), and [Shinjo Altansarnai](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shinjo-altansarnai) is attacking, she can react to a province being broken ([nested ability sequence](https://fiveringsdb.com/rules/reference#nested-ability-sequences)), before she is chosen as a target (step 5) and discarded (step 7).

If Endless Plains is broken as a cost of its ability, even though technically its controller broke it, any card effects referencing the attacker or the conflict breaking a province can still trigger (e.g. [Shinjo Altansarnai](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shinjo-altansarnai), [For Greater Glory](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/for-greater-glory), [Breakthrough](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/breakthrough)). The attacking player also can discard the dynasty card from the province.

If [Chasing the Sun](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/chasing-the-sun) or [Talisman of the Sun](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/talisman-of-the-sun) are used to move the contested ring to Endless Plains, the province's ability cannot be triggered, because an attack is not being declared.

text: This province gets +5 strength during [conflict-political] conflicts.
flavorText: "Our claim on these lands stretches back to the dawn of the Empire."
name: Ancestral Lands

text: Reaction: After this province is revealed – switch the contested ring with an unclaimed ring. (The conflict retains its type.)
name: Elemental Fury

rulings.text: Once the new ring becomes the contested ring, the attacking player takes all fate on it.
Elemental Fury does not change which ring was declared during conflict declaration.

errata.text: [Sample errata] Elemental Fury: At the start of the conflict — give one participating character +1 military and +1 political for the duration of this conflict.

text: This province gets +5 strength during [conflict-military] conflicts.
flavorText: "It would take an entire Moto horde to break through here."
name: Entrenched Position

text: Conflict Action: Draw 1 card.
flavorText: "Inari-no-Kami smiles upon these lands." - Aiko, priestess of Inari
name: Fertile Fields

text: Conflict Action: Gain 1 fate.
flavorText: The perfect garden maintains all five elements in harmony.
name: Manicured Garden

text: Conflict Action: Choose an attacking character – remove 1 fate from that character.
flavorText: "Appreciation of beauty is the beginning of wisdom." - Tao of Shinsei
name: Meditations on the Tao

text: Reaction: After this province is revealed – your opponent chooses and discards X cards from his or her hand. X is equal to the number of attacking characters.
name: Night Raid

text: During conflicts at this province, cancel all ring effects. (If this province breaks, resolve ring effects as normal.)
name: Pilgrimage

rulings.text: Pilgrimage cancels ALL ring effects, including those initiated by card effects (e.g. [Display of Power](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/display-of-power), [Akodo Toturi](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/akodo-toturi), [Doji Hotaru](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/doji-hotaru), [Guardian Kami](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/guardian-kami), [Kami Unleashed](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/kami-unleashed)).
Pilgrimage cancels ring effects before they can be interrupted (eg. [Restored Heirloom](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/restored-heirloom), [Asako Azunami](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/asako-azunami)).

text: Reaction: After this province is revealed – switch the conflict type. (The conflict retains its element.)
name: Rally to the Cause

rulings.text: When the conflict type is switched, any characters that cannot participate in the conflict of the new type (have a dash (–) for a skill, or have Pacifism attached) immediately go home bowed.
Changing the conflict type does not affect what other conflicts the attacker can declare. What matters is which conflict type was originally declared.

text: Action: During a conflict at this province, choose 2 participating characters – honor one of those characters and dishonor the other.
name: Shameful Display

rulings.text: Neither the honoring nor the dishonoring aspect of Shameful Display’s ability is dependent upon the other. Additionally, targets for the ability may be chosen even if one or both of those characters could be either honored or dishonored (you do not need to select which character is receiving which aspect when choosing targets). If Shameful Display honors/dishonors one character and fails to honor/dishonor the other, it has still resolved successfully, even though it failed to affect one of the chosen characters. That character is still a valid target for the ability as it could have been affected by either aspect at the time it was chosen.
Shameful Display can only be triggered if there are at least 2 eligible targets (participating characters that can each be either honored or dishonored; eligibility is assessed individually).

flavorText: The first to fight, the first to die.
name: Eager Scout

text: Action: While this character is participating in a conflict, choose another participating character – until the end of the conflict, that character gets +2[conflict-military] and +2[conflict-political] for each holding you control.
name: Hida Guardian

rulings.text: The bonus provided by Hida Guardian is fixed upon resolution. If the number of holdings subsequently changes, the bonus is not affected.
If Hida Guardian leaves the conflict after using his ability, the lasting effect will persist regardless.

text: Courtesy. (When this character leaves play, gain 1 fate.) Sincerity. (When this character leaves play, draw 1 card.)
name: Kaiu Envoy

text: This character cannot be declared as an attacker.
flavorText: Enemies learned quickly: it was never wise to give her reason to draw that awful blade.
name: Hiruma Yōjimbō

rulings.text: Hiruma Yōjimbō cannot be declared as an attacker, but can be moved to a conflict on the attacking side by card effects.

text: Action: While this character is participating in a conflict, if there is a holding in play – look at the top 2 cards of your conflict deck. Add 1 of those cards to your hand and place the other on the bottom of your conflict deck.
name: Shrewd Yasuki

rulings.text: Shrewd Yasuki can trigger if there is any holding in play, even one controlled by your opponent.

text: Action: Sacrifice this character. Choose a character – place 1 fate on that character.
flavorText: "You will have the time you need. Use it wisely."
name: Vanguard Warrior

text: While this character is defending, opponents' card effects cannot bow it or move it.
flavorText: Even when faced with such hardship, the Crab Clan has never wavered, standing steadfast in its duty to protect the southern border of the Empire.
name: Borderlands Defender

rulings.text: Borderlands Defender cannot be chosen to bow for [For Shame!](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/for-shame) or [Doji Gift Giver](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/doji-gift-giver).
Borderlands Defender can be bowed by the Water ring effect. Ring effects are not card effects. (revelant for [The Mountain Does Not Fall](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/the-mountain-does-not-fall))

text: Reaction: After an opponent passes on declaring a conflict – that player loses 1 honor.
flavorText: "Is that all you've got?"
name: Intimidating Hida

text: Reaction: After another character you control leaves play during a conflict – double this character's [conflict-military] skill until the end of the conflict.
name: Vengeful Berserker

rulings.text: Doubling modifiers apply after all +/- modifiers, regardless of the order of application of effects. So if you trigger Vengeful Berserker's ability, then give him a +X skill bonus, that bonus is doubled.

Vengeful Berserker does not double the bid value during a duel.

*During a duel, the bid on a player’s honor dial is applied
as a modifier independently of the normal process of
calculating modifiers, and is added after doubling and/or halving modifiers are calculated.*

text: Reaction: After this character wins a conflict as a defender, sacrifice it. Choose a non-unique attacking character – return that character to the top of its owner's deck.
name: Hida Tomonatsu

text: Action: Sacrifice a (friendly) character. Choose a character – ready that character.
flavorText: "The Shadowlands Taint can poison any soul. It is my duty to cleanse it from our ranks."
name: Steadfast Witch Hunter

rulings.text: Steadfast Witch Hunter can sacrifice itself to ready another character (the ability doesn't say choose *another* character).

text: Covert. (When this character attacks, choose one character without covert. That character cannot be declared as a defender.) Action: During a conflict in which this character is participating, if there is a holding in play – gain 1 fate.
name: Kaiu Shuichi

rulings.text: Kaiu Shuichi can trigger if there is any holding in play, even one controlled by your opponent.

text: While you have not lost a conflict this phase, cancel the effects of the first action ability your opponent triggers from a card during each conflict.
flavorText: "What need have I of glory to defend this Wall?"
name: Hida Kisada

rulings.text:
You cannot trigger an action ability to 'use up' Hida Kisada's cancel, if the triggered ability does not change the game state (eg. [Yōjin no Shiro](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/yojin-no-shiro) while defending, or [Shiro Nishiyama](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shiro-nishiyama) while attacking).

*"A triggered ability can only be initiated if its **effect** has the potential to change the game state on its own."* 

(Effect is denoted after the dash (–) in the ability's text.)
Hida Kisada can only cancel **Action:** abilities, not Reactions or Interrupts.

If your opponent uses an action, then Hida Kisada enters play (e.g. via Charge!), then your opponent uses another action, that last action will not be the first one during this conflict, so Kisada will not cancel it.
If the effects of an ability are canceled, the ability is still considered to have been used, and any costs have still been paid.

text: Action: Choose a card in one of your provinces – switch this holding with that card.
name: Borderlands Fortifications

rulings.text: Borderlands Fortifications cannot switch places with the stronghold.

text: Action: Sacrifice a (friendly) character – draw 1 card.
flavorText: "We speed our fallen to Meido, that they do not rise again."
name: Funeral Pyre

text: Action: During a conflict, bow this character. Choose another [clan-crane] character – that character gets +3[conflict-political] until the end of the conflict.
name: Asahina Artisan

flavorText: "Lean closer, that I might tell you something only the willows should hear..."
name: Doji Whisperer

text: Action: While this character is your only participating character in a conflict – honor this character.
flavorText: A samurai lives at all times three feet from death.
name: Brash Samurai

text: While this character is attacking alone, treat the defending province as if its printed text box were blank (except for Traits).
flavorText: They told him to be careful, but there was only one way to know for sure.
name: Cautious Scout

text: Action: While this character is participating in a conflict, give your opponent 1 fate. That opponent must choose a ready participating character he or she controls – bow that character.
name: Doji Gift Giver

text: Reaction: After this character is honored, choose a character – honor that character.
flavorText: For each of her accomplishments, she deferred the praise to those around her, and in this manner their loyalty was secured.
name: Savvy Politician

rulings.text: Savvy Politician triggers when it changes status from Dishonored to Ordinary, or from Ordinary to Honored  ("is honored" is different from "becomes honored").
Savvy Politician’s ability does not trigger if an honored status token is moved to it by a card ability. It only triggers if an effect “honors” it.

text: Action: Choose a facedown card in one of your provinces – turn that card faceup.
flavorText: "It's an ambush! Be ready!"
name: Daidoji Nerishma

text: Action: While this character is attacking, choose a character controlled by your opponent – move that character to this conflict.
flavorText: "I hope you are prepared to defend your words with steel!"
name: Doji Challenger

rulings.text: You cannot move a character to a conflict if that character cannot participate in that conflict (has a dash (–) for a skill, or a relevant constant ability, e.g. [Shiba Peacemaker](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shiba-peacemaker), [Pacifism](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/pacifism)).

text: Action: While this character is participating in a [conflict-political] conflict, if you count more current [conflict-political] skill in the conflict than your opponent – take 1 honor from that opponent.
name: Kakita Asami

rulings.text: When counting a current skill total, ignore bowed characters, but take Imperial Favor into account.
If a ***Magistrate*** makes some characters not count their skill toward the resolution of the conflict, they don't count for Kakita Asami's ability either.

text: Action: While this character is participating in a conflict, your opponent must choose a participating character he or she controls – challenge that character to a [conflict-military] duel. If this character wins, move each character not involved in the duel home. If it loses, send it home.
flavorText:
"For the coward, there is no life.
For the hero, there is no death."
name: Kakita Kaezin

rulings.text: Bowed characters do not contribute their skill whenever you count skill totals, but they still have their individual skill values (for duels, [Outwit](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/outwit)/[Rout](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/rout), etc.)

text: Sincerity. (When this character leaves play, draw 1 card.) Each honored [clan-crane] character you control gains sincerity.
name: Asahina Storyteller

rulings.text: A card can have multiple instances of the same keyword, but functions as if it has one instance of that keyword.

text: While this character is participating in a conflict, opponents cannot play events.
flavorText: "You wouldn't want to disgrace yourself in front of our distinguished guest, would you?"
name: Guest of Honor

rulings.text: The conflict ends only after all participating characters move home bowed, so Guest of Honor prevents your opponent from playing any events that react to winning/losing the conflict, breaking a province, resolving ring effects, or claiming the contested ring.

Declaring the type and element of the conflict, the attacked province, attacking characters, and covert targets, are all considered to be performed simultaneously, and they all share the same reaction window.

Guest of Honor will prevent an opponent from playing Reactions to a conflict being declared ([Mantra of Fire](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/mantra-of-fire) etc.), if she is already attacking, thus participating.

text: Reaction: After you claim a ring during a [conflict-political] conflict in which this character is participating – resolve that ring's effect.
flavorText: "Were it not for the rule of kō, we would be trapped in this game for eternity. We are nearly perfectly matched."
name: Doji Hotaru

rulings.text:
You can include 3 copies of Doji Hotaru (Core Set), or 3 copies of Doji Hotaru (Justice for Satsume), or any combination of each, so long as you include no more than 3 copies in total.

*No more than 3 copies of a single card by title can be included in any combination in a player's dynasty and conflict decks.*

text: Action: During the conflict phase – reveal the top card of your conflict deck. Until the end of the phase, you may play that card as if it were in your hand.
name: Artisan Academy

rulings.text: If the card revealed by Artisan Academy is moved to another game zone and is no longer the top card of the deck, the lasting effect applying to it expires, and you can no longer play it.

text: Limited. (No more than one limited card can be played by each player each round.) You cannot place fate on this character when it is played from one of your provinces.
name: Doomed Shugenja

text: Action: While this character is attacking, spend 1 fate to an unclaimed ring – honor this character.
flavorText: "What is wisdom?"
name: Togashi Initiate

text: Action: Search the top 5 cards of your conflict deck for an attachment, reveal it, and add it to your hand. Shuffle.
name: Agasha Swordsmith

rulings.text: Agasha Swordsmith can only find a card with the printed attachment cardtype. Monk characters that can be played as attachments ([Tattooed Wanderer](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/tattooed-wanderer), [Togashi Kazue](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/togashi-kazue)) are not attachments while they're out of play.

text: While this character is attacking alone, your opponent cannot declare more than one defender.
flavorText: Each new opponent showed her more of herself.
name: Mirumoto Prodigy

rulings.text: Only one character can be declared as a defender, but other characters can be played into or moved to the conflict.

text: Conflict Action: If you control an attachment on this character, choose a participating character without attachments - bow that character.
name: Niten Adept

text: This character gets +1[conflict-military] and +1[conflict-political] for each fate on each unclaimed ring.
flavorText: “What is not wisdom?”
name: Seeker of Enlightenment

rulings.text: Seeker of Enlightenment gets a bonus for each ring in the unclaimed ring pool. The currently contested ring does not count.

text: Reaction: After an opponent selects a ring with fate on it – place 1 fate on this character.
flavorText:
In heart, air.
In spirit, earth.
In sword, fire.
In body, water.
In mind, void.
name: Enlightened Warrior

rulings.text: Enlightened Warrior can only trigger after an opponent selects a ring with fate on it when declaring a conflict. Switching rings via card effects (e.g. [Know the World](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/know-the-world)) does not trigger him.

text: Action: While this character is participating in a [conflict-political] conflict, spend 1 fate to an unclaimed ring – look at your opponent's hand. Choose 1 of those cards and discard it. (Max 1 per conflict.)
name: Kitsuki Investigator

rulings.text: "Max 1 per conflict" means that you can only trigger one Kitsuki Investigator per conflict, even if you control several of them.

text: Action: While this character is participating in a conflict, choose a participating character controlled by your opponent – challenge that character to a [conflict-military] duel. If the loser of the duel has no fate on it, discard it. Otherwise remove 1 fate from it.
name: Mirumoto Raitsugu

rulings.text: Bowed characters do not contribute their skill whenever you count skill totals, but they still have their individual skill values (for duels, [Outwit](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/outwit)/[Rout](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/rout), etc.)

text: No attachments except Monk or Tattoo. Action: While this character is attacking, spend 1 fate to an unclaimed ring. Choose a Monk character or a character with a Monk attachment – ready that character.
name: Ascetic Visionary

text: Reaction: After you attach a Weapon attachment to this character – ready this character. (Limit twice per round.)
name: Niten Master

rulings.text: Niten Master can trigger whenever a ***Weapon*** attachment becomes attached to him (even if the effect says "move" etc.)
You can attach a third Restricted attachment to Niten Master, discard one of them so that there are only two left, then trigger Niten Master's ability.

text: Action: Choose a triggered ability printed on another character – this character gains that ability until the end of the phase. (Max 1 per round.)
flavorText: "What transpires in our mountains is a mere pebble against the avalanche that is coming."
illustrator: Shawn Ignatius Tan and Le Vuong
name: Togashi Yokuni

rulings.source: RRG "In Play and Out of Play"
rulings.text:
If Togashi Yokuni gains an ability that potentially allows him to enter a players hand of conflict cards (eg. [Adept of Shadows](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/adept-of-shadows)), the ability will not allow him to actually enter the conflict hand. He would instead enter his owner's discard pile.

*This is a reversal to a previous ruling.*

If Togashi Yokuni has [Way of the Dragon](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/way-of-the-dragon) attached, he cannot use his own ability twice (because it says "Max", not "Limit"), but he can use the gained ability twice.

"Max" is per ability per card title per player. If Togashi Yokuni gains the ability of [Kitsuki Investigator](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/kitsuki-investigator), both characters can use that ability this round (they have different titles). If Yokuni gains the ability of an opponent's Yokuni, he cannot use that ability (no infinite loop).
Togashi Yokuni can gain an ability printed on a character that is blanked by [Cloud the Mind](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/cloud-the-mind).
Togashi Yokuni cannot gain the ability of [Togashi Kazue](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/togashi-kazue). Kazue has a constant ability while she's a character, not a triggered ability.

Togashi Yokuni can only target characters in play, not in provinces or discard piles.

*Card abilities only interact with, and can only target, cards that are in play, unless the ability specifically refers to an out-of-play area or element.*

Togashi Yokuni targets an ability, not a character, so cards like [Shiba Yōjimbō](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shiba-yojimbo) and [Finger of Jade](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/finger-of-jade) do not protect from Yokuni's ability.

text: Reaction: After the conflict phase begins, each opponent who controls more characters than you must choose a character he or she controls with 1 or more fate on it – remove 1 fate from each chosen character.
name: Secluded Temple

text: If an opponent is more honorable than you, discard this character.
flavorText: Others might question his pride, assuming it would be his downfall. For him, it was a source of strength.
name: Obstinate Recruit

rulings.text: You can play Obstinate Recruit if you are less honorable than an opponent, but it will immediately be discarded.

text: Reaction: After 1 or more fate is placed on this character – gain 1 honor.
flavorText: She was destined to follow the path of her ancestors, the path of the true Go sage.
name: Ikoma Prodigy

rulings.text: Ikoma Prodigy can be triggered after fate is moved to the character.

flavorText: When the rage surged forth, none could stand against them.
name: Matsu Berserker

text: Forced Reaction: After the fate phase begins, if you have at least 5 more honor than an opponent – this character cannot be discarded or lose fate this phase.
name: Steadfast Samurai

text: Pride. (After this character wins a conflict, honor it. After this character loses a conflict, dishonor it.) Reaction: After this character enters play from a province – refill that province faceup.
name: Akodo Gunsō

rulings.text: The Pride keyword resolves immediately when the conflict winner is determined, before any Reactions to winning/losing a conflict can be used.

text: Reaction: After this character loses a conflict as an attacker, sacrifice this character. Choose a character controlled by your opponent – if that character has no fate on it, discard it. Otherwise, remove 1 fate from that character.
flavorText: "I shall redeem my name in service to my clan!"
name: Deathseeker

text: Action: While this character is participating in a conflict, if you are more honorable than your opponent – honor this character.
flavorText: "Only by looking to the past can we hope to conquer the future."
name: Venerable Historian

text: Action: During a conflict, bow this character. Choose a character in one of your discard piles – put that character into play in the conflict. If that character is still in play at the end of the conflict, return it to the bottom of its deck.
name: Kitsu Spiritcaller

rulings.text: When Kitsu Spiritcaller attempts to return a character to the bottom of the deck, that character can be prevented from leaving play by [Repreive](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/reprieve), [Stand Your Ground](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/stand-your-ground), etc.

text: Action: While this character is attacking, choose a character with equal or lower [conflict-military] skill than this character – bow that character.
flavorText: "Let me put it another way."
name: Lion's Pride Brawler

rulings.text:
Lion's Pride Brawler can bow a character that has a dash (-) for a military  skill.

*If a character has a dash for a skill value and a card ability
requires a numerical value for that skill, treat the card as if it
had an unmodifiable skill value of 0.*

text: Reaction: After this character enters play, if you control 3 or more other Bushi characters – place 2 fate on this character.
name: Matsu Beiona

text: During a conflict in which this character is participating, each other participating [clan-lion] character you control gets +1[conflict-military]. Reaction: After this character enters play – honor it.
name: Honored General

text: Reaction: After you lose a [conflict-political] conflict – put a Bushi character with printed cost 3 or lower into play from your dynasty discard pile or provinces.
name: Ikoma Eiji

rulings.text: Ikoma Eiji's ability cannot put characters straight into the conflict (specifically, you cannot combo it with [Deathseeker](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/deathseeker)).

Ikoma Eiji puts a character into play, rather than playing it.

As such, you do not have to pay fate for the character, and you cannot add additional fate to the character. This is the same whether the character comes from a province or your discard pile.

text: Reaction: After you claim a ring during a [conflict-military] conflict in which this character is participating – resolve that ring's effect.

text: Interrupt: When the effects of a triggered ability that targets a Shugenja you control would initiate – cancel those effects.
name: Shiba Yōjimbō

rulings.text: If the effects of an ability are canceled, the ability is still considered to have been used, and any costs have still been paid.

Shiba Yōjimbō cancels the effects of an ability when they initiate (in [step 6](https://fiveringsdb.com/rules/reference#initiating-abilities-playing-cards)) only if a Shugenja character has already been chosen as a target (in step 5; the ability's text must use the word "choose" before the dash (–) in the ability's text).

Some abilities choose targets when the effects resolve (in step 7; "choose" is after the dash, e.g. [Court Games](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/court-games), [Shosuro Miyako](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shosuro-miyako)), at that point it is too late to cancel their initiation.

Shiba Yojimbo cancels ***all*** effects of an ability that targets a Shugenja, even if that ability has multiple targets (e.g. [Shameful Display](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shameful-display), [Ide Tadaji](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/ide-tadaji])).

Shiba Yōjimbō cannot cancel the ability of [Shosuro Actress](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shosuro-actress) that targets a ***Shugenja*** in a discard pile, because Yōjimbō does not "see" traits on cards out of play.

Shiba Yōjimbō can be triggered when you have chosen your Shugenja as the target of an opponent's triggered ability, e.g. [Defend Your Honor](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/defend-your-honor) or [Vice Proprietor](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/vice-proprietor).

text: Interrupt: When a character would be honored or dishonored, choose another character controlled by the same player – that character receives the honor or dishonor instead.

rulings.text: Yound Rumormonger cannot redirect honor to an honored character (or dishonor to a dishonored character), because the ability would not affect that character at all, thus it would not be an eligible target.

If Young Rumormonger is used to change the recipient of dishonor when paying costs, the cost is still considered to be paid (e.g. [Forged Edict](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/forged-edict), [Spies at Court](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/spies-at-court), [Calling in Favors](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/calling-in-favors)).
Young Rumormonger can redirect the Fire ring effect to [Isawa Kaede](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/isawa-kaede).
Young Rumormonger is not bound by any targeting restrictions of the ability that he is interrupting.

If two *ordinary* characters would be simultaneously (dis)honored (by Pride or [The Art of Peace](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/the-art-of-peace)), Young Rumormonger can redirect (dis)honor from one to the other, so that only one character will end up (dis)honored, and the other one will be unaffected.

Young Rumormonger cannot interrupt a "fizzling" triggering condition (e.g. [Shameful Display](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shameful-display) attempting to honor an honored character) - that triggering condition is never created in the first place.
Young Rumormonger’s ability does not trigger if an honored or dishonored status token is moved to a character by a card ability. It only triggers if an effect “honors” or “dishonors” a character.

"***Seeker***/***Keeper*** only" is a deckbuilding restriction. You can take control of cards that do not match your role.

text: Reaction: After you play a character from your hand, your opponent must select one – discard 1 card at random from his or her hand or choose and dishonor a character he or she controls.

rulings.text: Your opponent must select an option that can change the game state (cannot choose to dishonor a dishonored character).

If your opponent selects the option to dishonor a character, the target is chosen during the resolution of the ability's effects. At that point it is too late to cancel the initiation of effects (e.g. via [Shiba Yōjimbō](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shiba-yojimbo)).

*Note that this is a non-standard template, usually you choose targets before the opportunity to cancel the initiation of effects (the word "choose" is usually printed before the dash (–) in the ability's text).*

Shosuro Miyako can be triggered after a character is played "as if it were in your hand" (e.g. via [Infiltrator](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/infiltrator), [Hidden Moon Dōjō](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/hidden-moon-dojo)).

Yogo Hiroue can move bowed characters to a conflict (the ability doesn't say "choose a *ready* character").

Yogo Hiroue can target participating characters, or characters that cannot participate in the conflict (eg. has a dash (–) for a skill, or a relevant constant ability, e.g. [Shiba Peacemaker](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shiba-peacemaker)).

text: Action: While this character is participating in a [conflict-political] conflict, choose a participating character controlled by your opponent – until the end of the conflict, that character gets –1[conflict-political] and is discarded if its [conflict-political] skill is 0. (Limit twice per round.)

rulings.text:
"Discard if political skill is 0" is a part of the lasting effect that applies until the end of the conflict. If Shoju's ability doesn't lower the character down to 0 skill immediately, but a subsequent effect during the same conflict does, the character will be discarded.

If a replacement effect is used to prevent the character from leaving play (e.g. [Reprieve](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/reprieve), [Stand Your Ground](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/stand-your-ground)) but does not concurrently increase its political skill above 0, Shoju's effect will immediately reapply, and the character will be discarded again.

If Bayushi Shoju leaves the conflict after using his ability, the lasting effect will persist regardless.

You can include 3 copies of Bayushi Shoju (Core Set), or 3 copies of Bayushi Shoju (Shoju's Duty), or any combination of each, so long as you include no more than 3 copies in total.

*No more than 3 copies of a single card by title can be included in any combination in a player's dynasty and conflict decks.*

Keeper Initiate's ability cannot be triggered while he is in play.

*If the pre-Then aspect of an effect does not successfully resolve in full, the post-Then aspect does not attempt to resolve.*

text: Action: During a conflict, remove 1 fate from this character – it gets +2[conflict-military] and +2[conflict-political] until the end of the conflict. (Limit twice per conflict.)

text: Action: Sacrifice this holding – draw 1 card.

text: Reaction: After this character enters play as a defender, choose a character – that character's abilities cannot be triggered during this conflict.
name: Hiruma Ambusher

rulings.text: Hiruma Ambusher prevents **Action:**, **Reaction:**, and **Interrupt:** abilities from being triggered. Constant abilities and keywords are not affected.

text: Action: During a conflict, sacrifice a (friendly) character – this character gets +3[conflict-military] until the end of the conflict.
flavorText: "We are the Wall."
name: Stoic Gunsō

text: Interrupt: When attached character would leave play – discard this attachment instead.
flavorText: There is one more thing that must be done.
name: Reprieve

rulings.text:
Reprieve’s effect can prevent a character from being sacrificed.

If that occurs during the payment of a cost, then that cost is not considered to have been paid as the sacrifice was prevented. If the sacrifice while paying an event’s cost is prevented, the effects do not initiate and that event remains unplayed in its owner’s hand.

If that occurs during the resolution of an effect, then nothing is dependent upon whether or not the sacrifice resolved successfully. In the same way that an event whose effects were canceled by [Voice of Honor](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/voice-of-honor) is still considered to have been played, a card like [Way of the Crab](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/way-of-the-crab) is still considered to have been played even if its effects are prevented by Reprieve.

text: Attach to a character you control. Limit 1 per character. Reaction: After an opponent plays a card during a conflict in which attached character is participating – that player loses 1 honor. (Unlimited.)
name: Watch Commander

rulings.text:
If a player plays a card whose effects result in Watch
Commander being discarded, its ability cannot be triggered
as it is no longer in play.

If a player plays a card whose effects result in the attached
character no longer participating in the conflict, Watch
Commander’s ability cannot be triggered as the triggering
condition is no longer met.
Players play cards from hand, or from provinces during the dynasty phase. Putting cards into play via card effects, or using abilities on non-Event cards, is not considered to be playing those cards.

The Reaction on Watch Commander can be triggered an Unlimited number of times per round, but only once per card played.

A reaction with a specified limit that enables it to be triggered more than once per round may only be initiated once each time its specified triggering condition occurs.

Watch Commander can still trigger even if the effects of an event have been canceled (eg. [Voice of Honor](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/voice-of-honor)), since the event has still been played.

*If the effects of an event card are canceled, the card is still considered to have been played, and it is still placed in its owner's discard pile.*

A second Watch Commander can be attached to a character.
The "Limit 1 per character" would then mean the first Watch Commander is discarded.

text: Attach to a character you control. Restricted. Action: While attached character is participating in a conflict, bow this attachment. Choose a participating character with lower [conflict-military] skill than attached character – return all fate on that character to its owner's fate pool.
name: Jade Tetsubō

rulings.text: *"Its owner"* refers to the owner of the targeted character.

text: Action: Choose an opponent, that player must select one – give you 1 fate or 1 honor.
flavorText: "Every clan must aid in the defense of Rokugan."
name: Levy

rulings.text: Your opponent must select an option that can change the game state (cannot choose to give fate is he has none).

text: Action: Shuffle a card in one of your unbroken provinces back into your dynasty deck. Choose a holding in your dynasty discard pile – put that holding into play in that province.
name: Rebuild

text: Action: Sacrifice a (friendly) [clan-crab] character. Choose an opponent – that player must sacrifice a character. (Max 1 per round.)
flavorText: Courage. Duty. Sacrifice.
name: Way of the Crab

rulings.text: Way of the Crab can be played during an action window in any phase of the round, including the Dynasty phase (you can use an eligible "**Action:**" ability instead of playing a character from a province), and the Fate phase (after characters with no fate are discarded).

Sacrificing a friendly character is the cost of Way of the Crab. By the time the ability's effects can be cancelled, costs have already been paid, and a friendly character has already been sacrificed.

Way of the Crab targets an opponent, not a character, so cards like [Above Question](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/above-question), [Shiba Yōjimbō](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shiba-yojimbo), and [Finger of Jade](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/finger-of-jade) do not protect from this event.

If the pre-dash sacrifice is prevented (e.g. by [Reprieve](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/reprieve)), the ability is prevented from initiating. The event remains unplayed in its owner's hand. A failed initiation attempt does not count towards Limits and Maximums. The opportunity to act is passed to the opponent.

text: Action: Choose a character - for this phase, that character does not bow as a result of conflict resolution while defending. (Max 1 per round.)
name: The Mountain Does Not Fall

text: While this character is participating in a conflict, characters cannot receive dishonored status tokens. Errata from RRG v11
flavorText: "There is no better armor than the edicts of Hantei-no-Kami."
name: Steward of Law

rulings.text: If a character “cannot receive dishonored status tokens,” then those tokens cannot be placed on or moved to that character. That character cannot be dishonored if it is ordinary.
Steward of Law prevents a dishonored status token from transferring to a Disguised character after it enters play via the Disguised keyword.

text: Covert. (When this character attacks, choose one character without covert. That character cannot be declared as a defender.) This character gets +3[conflict-political] while defending.
name: Political Rival

text: Attached character cannot be chosen as a target of an opponent's event.
flavorText: "Excuse me, samurai-san, but you must be mistaken! It could not possibly have been her."
name: Above Question

rulings.text:
If an opponent's event instructs a player to \*choose\* a target, the character with Above Question cannot be chosen. This also applies to events that instruct another player to choose targets (e.g. [Court Games](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/court-games)).

Above Question does not prevent attached character from being sacrificed for [Way of the Crab](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/way-of-the-crab) (that event doesn't instruct a player to \*choose\* a character, so it's non-targeting).

If Above Question is played on an opponent’s character, that opponent cannot choose the attached character as a target for their own events.

If the attached character is the only participating character controlled by that opponent, [Court Games](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/court-games)’s honoring effect cannot be selected, as the attached character cannot be chosen as a target.

text: Attached character gains: "Action: While this character is participating in a conflict, choose a participating character controlled by your opponent – challenge that character to a [conflict-military] duel. Instead of giving honor for this duel's bid, a player may choose and discard the required number of cards from hand. Bow the loser of the duel."
name: Duelist Training

rulings.text: Bowed characters do not contribute their skill whenever you count skill totals, but they still have their individual skill values (for duels, [Outwit](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/outwit)/[Rout](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/rout), etc.)
Duelist Training can be used to challenge a character to a duel, even if one or both of the participants are already bowed.

The general "once per round" limit applies to each ability granted by a specific copy of Duelist Training to a specific character.

One character can use abilities provided by multiple copies of Duelist Training (they are considered to be different abilities, since they are provided by different attachments). If the same copy of Dueling Training leaves and re-enters play, it is considered to be a new copy (leaving play resets the limit).

If a character uses the ability provided by Duelist Training, then the attachment is moved to another character (by [Giver of Gifts](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/giver-of-gifts) or [Calling in Favors](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/calling-in-favors)), that other character can use the ability (it is considered to be a different ability, since it is on a different character).

If a character uses the ability provided by Duelist Training, then the attachment is moved to another character, then back to the original character, that character cannot use the ability again (it is considered to be the same ability, since it is provided by the same attachment to the same character).

You can only choose and discard cards if you actually have enough cards.

You can either give honor or discard cards, but not a combination of both.

text: This attachment cannot be played during a conflict.
flavorText: The tides of fashion ebb and flow. Stay ahead of them, or sink beneath the waves of obscurity.
name: Height of Fashion

rulings.text: Height of Fashion can be played during the action window before a conflict (between steps 3.1 and 3.2), or during action windows in Draw, Fate, or Regroup phases.

text: Action: Reveal the top 4 cards of each player's conflict deck. Choose 1 revealed card owned by each player and add it to its owner's hand. Shuffle.
flavorText: He had refused twice, but could not bring himself to refuse a third time.

text: Interrupt: When the effects of an event would initiate, if you control more honored characters than each opponent – cancel those effects.
name: Voice of Honor

rulings.text: If the effects of an ability are canceled, the ability is still considered to have been used, and any costs have still been paid.

text: Action: Choose a [clan-crane] character you control – honor that character.

If the pre-dash sacrifice is prevented (e.g. by [Reprieve](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/reprieve)), the ability is prevented from initiating. The event remains unplayed in its owner's hand. A failed initiation attempt does not count towards Limits and Maximums. The opportunity to act is passed to the opponent.

They lack the “+” or “–” symbol before the value, so do not add their skills to the attached character. And only characters contribute their skill to a conflict (Tattooed Wanderer is considered to be an attachment rather than a character).

text:
Attach to a character you control. Limit 1 per character.
You may trigger each of attached character's triggered abilities an additional time each round (or specified period).
flavorText: Find your own path.
name: Way of the Dragon

rulings.text: Way of the Dragon does not work with abilities that say "Max X per [period]" ("Limit" applies to one particular card, "Max" applies to all cards with the same title).

Way of the Dragon increases the limit of the number of times an ability can be triggered. If a character triggers an ability the maximum allowed times, and Way of the Dragon subsequently leaves play (eg. [Let Go](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/let-go)), the limit will drop back to its printed amount. Attaching a new Way of the Dragon will again increase the limit, but will not allow an additional trigger of the ability, since the limit was previously reached.

Way of the Dragon does not use the word *printed*, so abilities gained from other cards (eg. [Duelist Training](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/duelist-training)) can also be triggered an additional time.

A second Way of the Dragon can be attached to a character.
The "Limit 1 per character" would then mean the first Way of the Dragon is discarded.

text: Action: Choose an attachment – discard it.
flavorText: The material world is but a distraction.
name: Let Go

text: Reaction: After an opponent declares a [element-fire] conflict, choose a Monk character or a character with a Monk attachment – place 1 fate on that character and draw 1 card.
name: Mantra of Fire

rulings.text: Reactions to a conflict being declared, to attackers committing to the conflict, and to a province being revealed, all share the same reaction window (first player gets the first opportunity to trigger their reaction).

Mantra of Fire can be played if your opponent declared a Fire conflict. Switching the contested ring (eg. [Elemental Fury](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/elemental-fury)) does not change which ring was declared.

text: Action: During a conflict – until the end of the conflict, characters in your opponent's home cannot trigger abilities.
flavorText: You have entered a state in which nothing can harm you.
name: Tranquility

rulings.text:
Tranquility applies a lasting effect *"Cannot use triggered abilities"* to each character that is at your opponent's home during the event's resolution. It does not create a blanket effect.

If a character enters the opponent's home after Tranquility resolves, it can trigger its abilities, because it was not affected.

If a character that was at the opponent's home when Tranquility resolved is moved to the conflict, it cannot trigger its abilities, because the lasting effect still applies to it.

text: Reaction: After a character you control wins a conflict alone - that character does not bow as a result of the conflict's resolution.
name: Indomitable Will

rulings.text: If you win a conflict with two participating characters, and use [Hisu Mori Toride](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/hisu-mori-toride) to sacrifice one of them, you can still play Indomitable Will - the "attacking alone" restriction is checked when the event is played.

text: Action: During a conflict, choose an attacking character with glory X or lower – bow that character. X is equal to the number of unrevealed provinces you control.
name: Mirumoto's Fury

rulings.text: The province stronghold also counts. Each player has 5 provinces.

text: Reaction: After you lose a [conflict-military] conflict – put this character into play from your hand.
flavorText: "When a samurai has said he will perform an action, it is as good as done." - Akodo's Leadership
name: Vengeful Oathkeeper

rulings.text: Vengeful Oathkeeper can only enter play at home via its ability, not in the conflict.

text: Action: While this character is attacking, your opponent must choose a participating character he or she controls – move that character home.
name: Master of the Spear

text: Action: Play this attachment from your conflict discard pile.
flavorText: "Oh blessed ancestors, hear my prayer! May the strength of your honor guide my steps and lead me to righteousness!"
name: Guidance of the Ancestors

rulings.text: You cannot trigger Guidance of the Ancestors from your discard pile if you cannot pay the attachment's cost (to get the ability canceled by [Hida Kisada](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/hida-kisada)).

Guidance of the Ancestors is still bound by the general "limit once per round" rule. So if you trigger its ability and it gets canceled by [Hida Kisada](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/hida-kisada), you cannot trigger it again this round.

Leaving play resets the limit. So if you play Guidance of the Ancestors from the discard pile, then it gets discarded from play (e.g. by [Let Go](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/let-go)), you can use its ability again this same round.

text: Restricted. (No more than two restricted attachments per character.) Reaction: After attached character wins a conflict – gain 1 honor.

text: Attach to a Bushi character you control. Attached character does not bow as a result of conflict resolution during [conflict-military] conflicts.

text: Reaction: After an opponent's card effect or a ring effect bows a character you control – ready that character.

The event remains unplayed in its owner's hand. A failed initiation attempt does not count towards Limits and Maximums. The opportunity to act is passed to the opponent.

text: Action: During a conflict, choose a [clan-lion] character – double that character's base [conflict-military] skill until the end of the conflict.
flavorText: "War is the most noble pursuit of a samurai. It gives him purpose and brings honor to his ancestors." - Akodo's Leadership
name: Way of the Lion

rulings.text: When you play two copies of Way of the Lion on the same character during a conflict, that character’s military skill will be doubled twice (to now be quadruple the original skill value). The new base skill value continues to double if additional copies are played.

Way of the Lion and [Bayushi Yunako](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/bayushi-yunako) interact in timestamp order (no layers). If you play Way of the Lion first, then trigger Yunako, the affected character will have a new base political skill equal to double its original base (printed) military skill.

text: Reaction: After you break a province during a [conflict-military] conflict – place 1 fate on each Bushi character on your side. (Max 1 per conflict.)
flavorText: "Our names shall never be forgotten!"

flavorText: The void is nothing. The void is everything.
name: Ishiken Initiate

rulings.text: Ishiken Initiate gets a bonus for each ring claimed by any player. The currently contested ring does not count.

text: While this character is attacking, the contested ring gains the [element-air] element. If this character wins the conflict as an attacker, you may choose which of its ring effects to resolve.
name: Seeker of Knowledge

rulings.text:
[Isawa Kaede](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/isawa-kaede) trumps Seeker of Knowledge.

The second sentence of the Seeker's text is redundant with the general rule for resolving effects of rings with multiple elements (see [RRG "Ring Effects"](https://fiveringsdb.com/rules/reference#ring-effects)). It does not carry any functional meaning.

text: Attached character gains pride. (After this character wins a conflict, honor it. After this character loses a conflict, dishonor it.)

rulings.text: The Pride keyword resolves immediately when the conflict winner is determined, before any Reactions to winning/losing a conflict can be used.
A card can have multiple instances of the same keyword, but functions as if it has one instance of that keyword.

text: Attach to a Shugenja character you control. Action: During a conflict, bow this attachment – opponents' characters cannot move to this conflict or be played from hand until the end of the conflict.

rulings.text:
Grasp of Earth does not prevent characters from being 'put into play' in a conflict.

Certain ***Monk*** characters (e.g. [Tattoed Wanderer](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/tattooed-wanderer), [Togashi Kazue](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/togashi-kazue)) can still be played as attachments in the conflict.

Grasp of Earth prevents opponent's characters from moving to the conflict even if they enter play after Grasp of Earth was triggered.

If attached character somehow ends up participating in a military conflict, it immediately goes home bowed.

text: Action: Choose a participating character – that character gets +X[conflict-military] and +X[conflict-political] until the end of the conflict. X is equal to the number of Shugenja characters you control.

rulings.text: The bonus provided by Supernatural Storm is fixed upon resolution. If the number of your Shugenja characters subsequently changes, the bonus is not affected.

text: Action: Choose a ring and an opponent – that player cannot declare conflicts of that ring's element this phase. (Max 1 per phase.)

rulings.text: If you choose a ring that gained other elements during a conflict (e.g. via [Seeker of Knowledge](seeker-of-knowledge), [Isawa Kaede](isawa-kaede)), the effect will prevent your opponent from initiating conflicts with any of those elements until the end of the phase.

text: Action: Choose a Shugenja character you control – bow or ready that character. Errata from the Imperial Law (2020-01-06)
flavorText: Water is ever changing. A gentle wave one moment and a torrent of force the next.
name: Against the Waves

text: Action: Switch a ring in your claimed ring pool with an unclaimed ring and gain all fate on that ring. (Retain the ring's conflict type.)
name: Know the World

rulings.text: Know the World does not trigger [Keeper Initiate](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/keeper-initiate) or [Seeker Initiate](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/seeker-initiate) (you do not claim the ring, you just place it into your claimed pool).
Rings have three disticts states - claimed, unclaimed, and contested. A contested ring is neither claimed not unclaimed. You cannot play Know the World to switch the contested ring.

*"(Retain the ring's conflict type)"* – When a ring is claimed, it is placed in your claimed pool indicating the type of the ongoing conflict. Relevant for cards like [Shiotome Encampment](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shiotome-encampment).

When you play Know the World, you take a ring from the unclaimed ring pool and place it in your claimed ring pool same side up as the ring that you are returning.

*Example: During a military conflict you claim the Air ring, and place it in your claimed ring pool military side up. Then you play Know the World and switch your claimed Air ring with an unclaimed Earth ring. You place the Earth ring to your claimed ring pool retaining the Air ring's conflict type - military side up.*

text: Reaction: After you lose an unopposed conflict – cancel the ring effect of that conflict. Then, you resolve that ring effect as if you had won the conflict as the attacking player. Claim that ring.
name: Display of Power

rulings.text:
When Display of Power is played, it produces a delayed effect that cancels the normal effects of ring resolution during step 3.2.6 “Resolve Ring Effects” and replaces it with “resolve the ring effect as if you had won the conflict as the attacking player.” Losing honor for unopposed and breaking the province will occur before resolving Display of Power’s effect.

- Should [Pilgrimage](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/pilgrimage) not be broken when Display of Power resolves, the delayed effect of Display takes precedence over Pilgrimage, canceling the normal resolution of “Resolve Ring Effects” and replacing it with the adjusted means of resolution. However, the Pilgrimage effect (“cancel all ring effects”) still cancels the Display of Power’s attempt to resolve the ring for the defender. The defender claims the ring.

- Because Display of Power interacts with the “Resolve Ring Effects” step rather than the specific effect chosen by the attacker, the attacker does not have the option to choose a specific ring effect to resolve when [Seeker of Knowledge](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/seeker-of-knowledge) is attacking (or whether they would like to resolve a ring effect at all). When Display of Power resolves the ring effect, the defender has the opportunity to choose which ring effect they would like to resolve instead, and may choose to not resolve the ring’s effect if they desire.
Resolving ring effects *as the attacking player* is optional. You may choose not to resolve the ring effect, and just claim the ring.
If a player plays Display of Power after [Isawa Kaede](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/isawa-kaede) wins the conflict as an attacker, that player gets to resolve all of the contested ring's effects.

Display of Power cannot be played by the attacking player.

*A conflict is unopposed if the attacking player wins the conflict and the defending player controls no participating characters at the time the conflict winner is determined.*
“*That ring effect*” and “*that ring*” refer to the ring effect that was cancelled (and its associated ring). Therefore, if no ring effect was cancelled, then the ability can do nothing more.
Once the normal ring effect for the conflict has been cancelled by Display of Power, you cannot play a second Display of Power as it would have nothing to cancel.

text: Action: Lose 1 honor – return this character to your hand.
flavorText: There are dozens of ways to hide, and only one way to be seen.

rulings.text: If a card leaves play and re-enters play during the same period, it is considered a new instance of the card and there is no memory of having used the ability during the specified period. (This rule also applies to any ability with no specified limit.)

If you take control of Adept of Shadows (e.g. via [Blackmail](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/blackmail) or [Shosuro Actress](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shosuro-actress)) and use its ability, it goes to its owner's hand.

If you take control of Court Mask (e.g. via [Calling in Favors](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/calling-in-favors)) and use its ability, it goes to its owner's hand.

text: Action: During a conflict, choose a participating non-[clan-scorpion] character – dishonor that character.
flavorText: To combat the liars, the thieves, and the traitors within the Great Clans, Bayushi's followers would have to lie, steal, and cheat in turn.
name: Way of the Scorpion

rulings.text: If a Scorpion character gains another clan affiliation (e.g. via [Seal of the Crab](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/seal-of-the-crab)), it is still a Scorpion character, and thus not an eligible target for Way of the Scorpion.

text: Action: Dishonor a (friendly) character. Choose an opponent's attachment – take control of that attachment and attach it to that character, if able. Otherwise, discard the attachment.
name: Calling in Favors

rulings.text: If Calling in Favors is used to dishonor a character and take control of an opponent's attachment that is attached to that same character (e.g. [Cloud the Mind](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/cloud-the-mind), [Fiery Madness](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/fiery-madness)), the attachment stays in play.
If [Young Rumormonger](young-rumormonger) redirects the dishonor, the cost is still considered to be paid, and the character that was originally selected for dishonor gets the attachment.

If you take control of an opponent's unique attachment (e.g. [Talisman of the Sun](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/talisman-of-the-sun)), neither player can play another copy of that attachment (because each player either owns or controls a copy in play).
If you control a unique attachment, you can attempt to take control of another copy of that attachment. Since you cannot take control of it, it will be discarded.
"***Seeker***/***Keeper*** only" is a deckbuilding restriction. You can take control of cards that do not match your role.

text: Conflict Action: If the bid on your honor dial is higher than an opponent's, choose a dishonored character on their side – discard that character.
flavorText: "But scorpion! Now we will both die!"
name: I Can Swim

rulings.text:
Effects that modify the value of the honor bid (e.g. [Contingency Plan](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/contingency-plan), [Bayushi Manipulator](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/bayushi-manipulator)) only affect the current bid, the setting on the dial is not physically adjusted.

Duels physically adjust the setting on the dial, same way as honor bids during the Draw phase.

text: Action: During a conflict, choose up to 2 [clan-scorpion] characters with total printed cost 6 or lower from your hand and/or provinces – put those characters into play as participating characters.
name: Ambush

rulings.text: When playing Ambush, you need to reveal the characters that you're going to put into play before your opponent gets the opportunity to cancel the event's effects.
You cannot place additional fate on characters when you put them into play by card effects. You can only do that when you play characters from provinces or from hand.

text: Play only if you are less honorable than your opponent. Action: During a conflict, choose a character with printed cost 2 or lower controlled by your opponent – take control of that character until the end of the conflict.
name: Blackmail

rulings.text: If you Blackmail a character that has already used its ability, you can use it again. Limits and Maximums are player-specific.
If you take control of a character that has an attachment that says "*Attach only to characters you control*", that attachment is immediately discarded.
If you take control of a non-participating character, it goes to your home, it does not enter the conflict.
When you take control of a character, you do not take control of any attachments attached to that character.

If you take control of a participating character that cannot participate in the current conflict on your side (e.g. [Shiba Peacemaker](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shiba-peacemaker)), it immediately bows and goes home.
A Blackmailed character is considered to be *friendly*, and can be sacrificed to pay the cost of certain abilities (e.g. [Noble Sacrifice](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/noble-sacrifice)).
"***Seeker***/***Keeper*** only" is a deckbuilding restriction. You can take control of cards that do not match your role.

text: Reaction: After this character enters play – look at a facedown province.
flavorText: Chase the sun. Discover the world.
name: Iuchi Wayfinder

rulings.text: "Look at" is not the same as "reveal", so a province's "When revealed..." ability can no be triggered.

text: Action: During a conflict, spend 1 fate. Choose a character you control – move that character to the conflict.
name: Ide Messenger

text: Attach to a character you control. Attached character gains Cavalry. Action: During a conflict, bow this attachment – move attached character to the conflict.
name: Favored Mount

text: Reaction: After attached character commits to a conflict or moves to a conflict – draw 1 card. (Limit twice per round.)
name: Spyglass

rulings.text:
Reactions to a conflict being declared, to attackers committing to the conflict, and to a province being revealed, all share the same reaction window (first player gets the first opportunity to trigger their reaction).

If a character with Spyglass attacks [Restoration of Balance](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/restoration-of-balance), the first player gets the first opportunity to trigger reactions. If the attacker is the first player, they can pass on reactions, wait for the defender to use the province, then trigger Spyglass.
The reaction on Spyglass can still be triggered, even if the attached character has been removed from the conflict before the controlling player's opportunity to take the reaction.

text: Cavalry character only. X is equal to the number of unclaimed rings.
name: Born in War

rulings.text: During a conflict, the contested ring does not count as unclaimed.

text: Reaction: After the resolution of your first conflict this phase as the attacking player, if that conflict broke a province – immediately declare your second conflict for this phase.
name: Breakthrough

rulings.text: Breakthrough is played after participants of the first conflict go home bowed, and a new conflict starts immediately. There is no action window between conflicts in which you could ready a character in time for it to be declared as an attacker (e.g. [Border Rider](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/border-rider), [I Am Ready](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/i-am-ready)).
If you pass your first conflict opportunity, you cannot declare your second conflict, so you cannot play Breakthrough.
If [Waning Hostilities](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/waning-hostilities) was played this phase, Breakthrough cannot be played.
Breakthrough does not force your opponent to pass their opportunity to declare a conflict. It simply changes the order of how conflicts are declared (from the regular alternating between players).

text: Action: During a [conflict-political] conflict, lose 1 honor – change the conflict type to [conflict-military].
flavorText: "Now you must heed our demands!"
name: Captive Audience

rulings.text: When the conflict type is switched, any characters that cannot participate in the conflict of the new type (have a dash (–) for a skill, or have [Pacifism](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/pacifism) attached) immediately go home bowed.
Changing the conflict type does not affect what other conflicts the attacker can declare. What matters is which conflict type was originally declared.
Losing 1 honor is the cost of Captive Audience. By the time the ability's effects can be cancelled, costs have already been paid, and honor has already been lost.

text: Action: Remove 1 fate from a (friendly) [clan-unicorn] character – ready that character.
name: I Am Ready

text: Interrupt: When you would pass the first player token – keep it instead.
flavorText: One cannot capture the wind.
name: Way of the Unicorn

text: [conflict-military] Conflict Action: Choose up to 6 printed cost worth of Cavalry characters in your dynasty discard pile - put those characters into play in the conflict.
name: Cavalry Reserves

text: Restricted. (No more than two restricted attachments per character.)
flavorText:
Immortal steel waits
Venerated divine blade
The fires of the soul

text: Restricted. (No more than two restricted attachments per character.)
flavorText: At times, the ornamentation is the point. At others, it is merely a distraction.
name: Ornate Fan

text:
Play only if you control a Shugenja character.

Attached character loses all printed abilities.
name: Cloud the Mind

rulings.text: Only printed text is blanked. Text gained from another source is not blanked.

Cloud the Mind can only be played if you control a Shugenja character **in play**.

*Card abilities only interact with, and can only target, cards that are in play, unless the ability specifically refers to an out-of-play area or element.*
If Cloud the Mind is attached to a character that has already triggered its ability, the effects of that ability are not blanked/negated.

text: Conflict Action: Lose 3 honor. Choose a character with printed cost 2 or lower – discard that character. (Max 1 per round.)
name: Assassination

rulings.text: Losing 3 honor is the cost of Assassination. By the time the ability's effects can be cancelled, costs have already been paid, and honor has already been lost.

text: Action: During a conflict, choose a participating character – that character gets +2[conflict-military] until the end of the conflict. You may lose 1 honor to resolve this ability twice. (Max 1 per conflict.)
name: Banzai!

rulings.text:
When Banzai! is played, a player can choose to cancel Banzai!’s entire card effect, just the second part of its effect, or none of its effect.

- When Banzai!’s effect initiates during the step 6 of playing the card (see [Playing Cards](https://fiveringsdb.com/rules/reference#initiating-abilities-playing-cards)), the effects that initiate are: “you may lose 1 honor, the chosen character gets +2, and if you lost 1 honor resolve this ability twice.” If Banzai!’s effects are canceled at this time, none of those things happen and the card’s effects do not resolve at all.

- If those effects resolve, and the 1 honor is lost, “resolve this ability twice” causes the ability’s resolution to return to step 5 (“choose a participating character”). This causes the effects to initiate a second time, and thus they can be canceled a second time to prevent the second +2 and optional honor loss.
Losing 1 honor to "resolve the ability twice" is an optional part of the card's effect, not a cost.

text: Reaction: After honor dials are revealed – increase or decrease the value of your bid by 1 (to a minimum of 0).
flavorText: "The only thing that is certain is that something will go wrong." - Bayushi's Lies
name: Contingency Plan

rulings.text: Effects that modify the value of the honor bid only affect the current bid, the setting on the dial is not physically adjusted.

text: [conflict-political] Conflict Action: Select one - either choose and honor a participating character you control, or your opponent chooses and dishonors a participating character they control. (Max 1 per conflict.)
name: Court Games

rulings.text:
If you select the option to make your opponent dishonor a character, the target is chosen during the resolution of the ability's effects. At that point it is too late to cancel the initiation of effects (e.g. via [Shiba Yōjimbō](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shiba-yojimbo)).

*Note that this is a non-standard template, usually you choose targets before the opportunity to cancel the initiation of effects (the word "choose" is usually printed before the dash (–) in the ability's text).*

text: Conflict Action: If you control a participating Courtier character, choose a participating character controlled by your opponent. Your opponent must select one – dishonor the chosen character or bow it.
name: For Shame!

rulings.text: Your opponent must select an option that can change the game state (cannot choose to bow a bowed character, or dishonor a dishonored character).

text: Action: If you have composure (you have composure if your honor bid is lower than an opponent's), choose a character with printed cost 3 or higher – place 1 fate on that character.
name: Good Omen

rulings.text:
Effects that modify the value of the honor bid (e.g. [Contingency Plan](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/contingency-plan), [Bayushi Manipulator](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/bayushi-manipulator)) only affect the current bid, the setting on the dial is not physically adjusted.

Duels physically adjust the setting on the dial, same way as honor bids during the Draw phase.

text: Reaction: After you win a [conflict-political] conflict, dishonor a (friendly) participating character – discard 2 cards at random from your opponent's hand. (Max 1 per conflict.)
name: Spies at Court

text: Action: During a [conflict-military] conflict, choose a character in one of your provinces – put that character into play in the conflict.
flavorText: A samurai must always be ready. Ready to fight. And ready, if need be, to die.
name: Charge!

rulings.text: You cannot place additional fate on characters when you **put** them **into play** by card effects. You can only do that when you **play** characters from provinces or from hand.

You cannot put a character into play participating in a conflict if that character cannot participate in that conflict (has a dash (–) for a skill, or a relevant constant ability, e.g. [Shiba Peacemaker](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/shiba-peacemaker), [Pacifism](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/pacifism)).

text: Reaction: After you win a [conflict-military] conflict by 5 or more skill, choose a participating character – discard that character. (Max 1 per conflict.)
name: Fallen in Battle

rulings.text: Reactions to winning/losing a conflict are played after the skill you won by is fixed. Playing Fallen in Battle will not help you win unopposed or break a province.

text: Action: During a conflict, choose an opponent's character with lower [conflict-political] skill than a participating Courtier character you control – move the chosen character home.
name: Outwit

rulings.text: Outwit can target a character with a dash (–) for political skill (treat dash as 0).

Bowed characters do not contribute their skill whenever you count skill totals, but they still have their individual skill values (for duels, [Outwit](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/outwit)/[Rout](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/rout), etc.)

text: Action: During a conflict, choose an opponent's character with lower [conflict-military] skill than a participating Bushi character you control – move the chosen character home.
name: Rout

rulings.text: Bowed characters do not contribute their skill whenever you count skill totals, but they still have their individual skill values (for duels, [Outwit](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/outwit)/[Rout](https://fiveringsdb.com/card/rout), etc.)
Rout can target a character with a dash (–) for military skill (treat dash as 0).

text: Increase your deckbuilding influence value by 3. Reaction: After you win an [element-air] conflict as the defending player – gain 1 fate.
name: Keeper of Air

text: You may replace 1 province of any element with an additional [element-air] province while deckbuilding. Reaction: After an [element-air] province you control is revealed – gain 1 fate.
